title: Why hasn't my deposit been credited? | OKX United States
content: Getting started Why hasn't my deposit been credited? Published on Sep 1, 2023 Updated on Mar 3, 2026 6 min read 13,721 Important Notice: Due to a platform system upgrade, some deposit addresses were updated on March 27, 2025. Before transferring any assets, please check the latest deposit address and supported network on the OKX deposit page. If you've used an OKX deposit address before, don't worry. Previously used deposit addresses will remain valid for a short period of time, and deposits sent to old addresses during this period will not be lost. That said, if you've saved or whitelisted an OKX deposit address, please update it as soon as possible to avoid any issues later. Please also be aware that some tokens are no longer supported on certain networks. For example, USDT deposits via the BSC network are not supported on OKX, although BNB deposits on the BSC network are still supported. Delayed from block confirmation You can check if you've input the correct deposit information and your transaction status on the blockchain. If your transaction is on the blockchain, you can check if your transaction reaches the required confirmation numbers. You will receive your deposit amount once it reaches the required confirmation numbers. If your deposit can't be found on the blockchain, you can reach out to your corresponding platform's customer support for assistance. Deposit different cryptos Before you submit a deposit request, make sure that you've selected the crypto supported by the corresponding platform. Otherwise, it could lead to deposit failure. Select the crypto that is supported by the corresponding platform Incorrect address and network Before you submit a deposit request, make sure that you've selected the network supported by the corresponding platform. Otherwise, it could lead to deposit failure. Select the deposit network which is supported by the corresponding platform in the Deposit network field For example, you would like to deposit ETH to a BTC address that is not compatible. This can lead to deposit failure. In such a case, you can reach out to our customer support for assistance. Incorrect or missing tag/memo/comment The crypto that you want to deposit might require filling in a memo/tag/comment. You can find it on our deposit page. You can find the Tag on our deposit page If you find a tag, enter the tag in the Tag field on the corresponding platform's withdrawal page. If you can't find the Tag field on the corresponding platform, you can reach out to the corresponding platform's customer support for assistance. Note: if you enter an incorrect/missing tag, it could lead to deposit failure. In such a case, you can reach out to our customer support for assistance. Deposit to smart contract addresses Before you submit a deposit request, make sure that you've selected the deposit contract address supported by the corresponding platform. Otherwise, it could lead to deposit failure. Make sure the deposit contract address is supported by the corresponding platform Blockchain reward deposits The profit from mining can only be deposited into your wallet. You can only deposit the rewards into our account once it is deposited into your wallet, as we don't support blockchain reward deposits. Combined deposits When you want to do a deposit, make sure you submit only one deposit request each time. If you submit multiple requests into a single deposit transaction, you'll not receive your deposit. In such a case, you can reach out to our customer support for assistance. Fail to reach the minimum deposit amount Before you submit a deposit request, make sure that you deposit at least the minimum amount which you could find on our deposit page. Otherwise, it could lead to deposit failure. Make sure you deposit at least the minimum deposit amount shown on our Deposit page We're unable to process any deposit amount less than the minimum, nor are we able to reverse or refund the transaction. Note: you can find the minimum deposit amount on the asset’s deposit details page. Unsupported or deactivated receiving platform If your deposit was sent to an address belonging to another platform that has been deactivated or no longer supports deposits, the funds may not be credited to your OKX account. In some cases, the assets may be returned to the originating wallet instead of your own account. Otherwise, you'll need to contact the receiving platform's customer support to request recovery. Deposits of non-tradable assets If you deposit assets that are not supported for trading or deposits on OKX, the funds may not be credited. Please check whether the deposited asset is tradable and supported on OKX before initiating a deposit. For more information, refer to the article on non-tradable assets and learn more about how to withdraw untradeable assets here . Discrepancy between deposited and withdrawn amounts When initiating an on-chain withdrawal, a network fee is charged. As a result, the deposited amount may be lower than the withdrawal amount due to this fee. We recommend contacting the sender to confirm the actual withdrawal amount and applicable fees. If none of the above applies to you, please contact our customer support by clicking the button below: Contact us Disclaimer This content is provided for informational purposes only and may cover services and products that are not available in your state. See OKX's US Risk & Compliance Disclosures for more information. It is not intended to provide (i) investment advice or an investment recommendation, (ii) an offer, solicitation, or inducement to buy, sell or hold digital assets, or (iii) financial, accounting, legal or tax advice. Crypto/digital assets, including stablecoins and NFTs, are subject to market volatility, involve a high degree of risk, can lose value, and are not FDIC insured. You should carefully consider whether trading or holding crypto/digital assets is suitable for you in light of your financial condition. Please consult your legal/tax/investment professional for questions about your specific circumstances. title: How do I make a withdrawal? (web) | OKX United States
content: How do I make a withdrawal? (web) Published on Aug 22, 2023 Updated on Feb 24, 2026 3 min read 1,565 This information may not apply to all customers Log in to check if the products, features, rules, and terms in this article apply to you. When depositing or withdrawing crypto, make sure the network used matches the network of the destination; otherwise, your assets may be lost. After you submit a withdrawal request, it will go through 4 statuses: Withdrawal Requested, Pending, Withdrawing, Completed. When the withdrawal details page shows Completed, it means the assets have left the platform. You will need to contact the support team of the receiving platform or wallet to verify receipt. There are 2 withdrawal methods on OKX: On-chain withdrawal : Transfer crypto via network to a wallet or an exchange. Internal transfer : Transfer crypto instantly with zero fees to OKX users. Getting started Log in to your account at okx.com , go to the Assets and select Withdraw . On-chain withdrawal Select crypto to withdraw and on-chain withdrawal method Select crypto and on-chain withdrawal method to withdraw Complete the withdrawal details and select Next Fill in the Address Note: you can enable allowlist mode to protect your assets. Select the withdrawal network from available options Note: normally, the system will automatically match the network to the selected address. If multiple networks are available, you need to ensure that the withdrawal network selected matches the deposit network used in other exchanges or wallets to avoid potential losses. Insert Withdrawal amount Note: the withdrawal amount should be more than the minimum amount but can't exceed the limit based on your verification level. Network fee Note: the network fee can vary between networks and is determined by blockchain. Fill in withdrawal details on the on-chain withdrawal page Complete the 2FA verification and select Confirm, your withdrawal order will be submitted Note: some cryptos (for example, XRP) may require tags to complete the withdrawal, which is usually a sequence of numbers. It's necessary to fill in both the withdrawal address and the tag, otherwise, the withdrawal will be lost. The withdrawal submitted pop-up notice will appear once the submission is done Internal transfer Select crypto to withdraw and internal (free) withdrawal method Select crypto and internal withdrawal method to withdraw Complete the withdrawal details and select Next Select recipient and insert recipient info (Phone number / Email / Sub-account / UID) Note: the withdrawal recipient needs to be a verified user, learn more on how you can be verified here . Insert Withdrawal amount Note: the withdrawal amount should be more than the minimum amount but can't exceed the limit based on your verification level. Fill in withdrawal details on the internal withdrawal page Complete the 2FA verification and select Confirm, your withdrawal order will be submitted Note: if you change your mind, you can cancel the request within 1 minute and no fees will be charged. Getting started On-chain withdrawal Internal transfer

title: How do I find my deposit address and tag/memo/comment? | OKX United States
content: How do I find my deposit address and tag/memo/comment? Published on Aug 22, 2023 Updated on Mar 22, 2026 4 min read 67,955 When you deposit cryptocurrency to our platform from third-party exchanges or non-custodial wallets, you'll need a deposit address, and sometimes even a tag/memo from us depending on the cryptocurrency you are depositing. Here's where you can find the address and tag/memo when depositing. On the app Open your OKX app, and select Deposit crypto Select the crypto you want to deposit, then select the designated deposit network You'll see your Deposit address and tag/memo/comment if it's required Note: if your selected crypto comes with a tag/memo/comment, there is a Deposit reminder that you need to read and acknowledge before viewing the deposit details. You can select Copy to copy both the deposit address and tag/memo/comment or select Share to share them with your friends and family On the web Log in to your account and select Deposit Select the crypto you want to deposit, then select the designated deposit network You'll see your Deposit address and tag/memo/comment if it's required Note: if your selected crypto comes with a tag/memo/comment, there is a Deposit reminder that you need to read and acknowledge before viewing the deposit details. You can select Copy to copy both the deposit address and tag/memo/comment and share them with your friends and family How do I manage my deposit address? Can I delete a deposit address? Created deposit addresses can’t be modified or deleted. You can add up to 20 addresses for different cryptos and networks. How do I switch between multiple deposit addresses? If you have multiple deposit addresses, all of them can receive funds. Go to Deposit > Deposit crypto > switch address Switch to your desired addresses How do I add a new deposit address? On the deposit page, select Address > Add address Go to address book page to add new address How do I edit the name of a deposit address? On the deposit page, select Address > Edit > Edit the deposit address name Select the edit from the address book page to edit How do I change my deposit address? Select the crypto asset and corresponding network Under the deposit details section, select Address In the regular address section, select Generate new address Note: You can generate up to 20 addresses. Once generated, the addresses can't be deleted. Note: Copy the deposit address and tag/memo/comment, paste it on the withdrawal page of the platform of your choice and follow the instructions to complete your deposit. Make sure the withdrawal network of your selected platform matches the deposit address on our platform. USDT deposits via the BSC network aren't supported, please select a different network to avoid potential asset loss. Not every cryptocurrency and network requires a tag/memo/comment. If it's required, make sure you fill in both the deposit address and tag/memo/comment to avoid any potential losses; if there is no tag/memo/comment required, the deposit address alone will be sufficient. The process for sub-accounts to find deposit addresses is the same as for the main account. If your deposit address appears different, this may be due to the system upgrade or the presence of multiple available addresses, which is normal. To reduce operational risk, please consistently use the same address when possible. title: Why do I need to fill in tags (tag/memo) when I withdraw or deposit certain cryptos? | OKX United States
content: Why do I need to fill in tags (tag/memo) when I withdraw or deposit certain cryptos? Published on Aug 22, 2023 Updated on Mar 4, 2026 2 min read 413 Tag (tag/memo) is an unique digital string used to identify deposit address. It ensures the funds are credited to the correct address. OKX distributes different tags to users’ deposit addresses of certain cryptos requested by the network. Tag exist in pairs, one for each deposit address. You need to fill out both the deposit address and tag/memo before submitting a request. If this is not done, the funds may not be credited to the correct address. Note: deposit tag/memo are typically 7–8 digits long. Please refer to the tag/memo number displayed on the deposit page and enter it exactly as shown. For example, you can find both the deposit address and tag/memo on the deposit page in the screenshot below: Deposit page with both deposit address and tag How do I fill in the tag/memo? To withdraw crypto that requires a tag or memo, follow these steps: Open the OKX App, then go to Assets > Withdraw crypto, and select the crypto you want to withdraw Select Exchanges or wallets and your preferred Network Complete the withdrawal details including the tag/memo Note: it's necessary to fill in both the withdrawal address and the tag, otherwise, the withdrawal will be lost. Then, submit the request and complete the necessary password or verification steps. Important note: If you’re withdrawing to a saved address from your address book, the Tag/Memo field may not appear, as it was already saved when the address was added. If the Tag/Memo is entered incorrectly, the funds may not be credited successfully. In this case, please click the Get help button below to submit feedback and contact our support team. If you're having issues with your tag/memo, click the button below. Get help with tag/memo issues Note: please follow the bot's instructions and complete all required fields in the ticket form (including email, TXID, token type, amount, and network) to help us review your request efficiently. How do I fill in the tag/memo? Important note:

title: What should I do if I forget to fill in tag/memo? | OKX United States
content: What should I do if I forget to fill in tag/memo? Published on Oct 15, 2024 Updated on Mar 4, 2026 2 min read 109 This may result in funds not being credited to the correct address. You can have the deposit/withdrawal details ready and contact our customer support immediately by starting with our AI chatbot: you can find it in the OKX Support Center on the website or in the app. You can also contact our customer support through chatbot. Note: the deposit/withdrawal details include: cryptocurrency, amount, the correct tag, the incorrect tag you filled in and the hash value of the transaction. Learn more: Why do I need to fill in tags (tag/memo) when I withdraw or deposit certain cryptos? If you're having issues with your tag/memo, click the button below. Get help with tag/memo issues Note: please follow the bot's instructions and complete all required fields in the ticket form (including email, TXID, token type, amount, and network) to help us review your request efficiently.

title: Why is my deposit not credited even when all information is correct? | OKX United States
content: Why is my deposit not credited even when all information is correct? Published on Oct 17, 2024 Updated on Feb 12, 2026 2 min read 1,789 When you deposit funds to our platform, if the receiving address, deposit network, and token contract are correct, and there is a record on the blockchain, but the funds haven't arrived, this may be due to the following reasons: There's a delay in syncing the blockchain to the platform: for the token you deposited, there may be a delay in syncing from the blockchain to the platform’s wallet, which can result in the deposit not being credited and not showing up in your assets. We recommend contacting customer support directly to check the specific reason. The deposit network for this token is currently closed: for some tokens, the network may temporarily suspend deposits, which can prevent the deposit from being credited. In such cases, the asset will not display in your platform account. Rest assured, assets that have already been recorded on the blockchain will not be lost. Once the network reopens for deposits, the platform will process it promptly. You can refer to the deposit page for the estimated recovery time and check back later.
